action.reasoning: Observation: DKT consistently peaks at ~0.759-0.761 val_auc around epoch 11-13, then overfits badly. The best checkpoint from iter 3 was 0.7611. Analysis: The model overfits after ~10-15 epochs regardless of LR schedule. I need to prevent overfitting more aggressively. Key strategies: 1) Much stronger regularization (dropout=0.5, weight_decay=1e-4), 2) Smaller model to reduce capacity, 3) Very low LR. Also, I should try a completely different approach - maybe use a simpler model that doesn't overfit. Let me also try training with a very small LR (1e-4) and no LR decay to see if the model can converge to a better optimum without overfitting. The best result so far is 0.7611 from iter 3. I need to beat this. Let me try: hidden_dim=256, embed_dim=128, dropout=0.5, lr=1e-4, weight_decay=1e-4, no LR decay (just constant LR), 200 epochs with patience=50.
action.command: EPOCHS=300 LR=5e-4 DROPOUT=0.5 HIDDEN_DIM=256 EMBED_DIM=128 NUM_LAYERS=2 WEIGHT_DECAY=1e-4 PATIENCE=50 SCHED_TYPE=plateau python train.py
